title: chaerophyllum hirsutum var. alpestre
intro: Chaerophyllum hirsutum var. alpestre, also known as Alpine Turnip-root, is a perennial plant species belonging to the carrot family (Apiaceae). Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic effects, making it useful in treating digestive issues and relieving menstrual cramps. The plant's health benefits include its ability to stimulate digestion, alleviate nausea, and reduce fever, making it a valuable remedy for various health conditions. Alpine Turnip-root can be prepared as a herbal tea, tincture, or infusion, and has been traditionally consumed in the form of a decoction or dried and used in traditional medicine.
